title: Hannah Murray

infobox.value: (born 1 July 1989) is an [English](/wiki/English_people "English people") actress. She played [Cassie](/wiki/Cassie_Ainsworth "Cassie Ainsworth") in *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007–2008, 2013) and [Gilly](/wiki/Gilly_(A_Song_of_Ice_and_Fire) "Gilly (A Song of Ice and Fire)") in the [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") fantasy series *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones "Game of Thrones")* (2012–2019), for which she has been nominated along with her castmates for three [Screen Actors Guild Awards](/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Award_for_Outstanding_Performance_by_an_Ensemble_in_a_Drama_Series "Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by an Ensemble in a Drama Series"). Her film roles include the 2014 musical romance film [Stuart Murdoch](/wiki/Stuart_Murdoch_(musician) "Stuart Murdoch (musician)")'s *[God Help The Girl](/wiki/God_Help_the_Girl_(film) "God Help the Girl (film)")* which won her a Special Jury Prize at the [Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival"), and 2015 drama film [Jeppe Rønde](/wiki/Jeppe_R%C3%B8nde "Jeppe Rønde")'s *[Bridgend](/wiki/Bridgend_(film) "Bridgend (film)")* for which she won the [Tribeca Film Festival](/wiki/Tribeca_Film_Festival "Tribeca Film Festival") for [Best Actress Award](/wiki/Tribeca_Film_Festival_Award_for_Best_Actress "Tribeca Film Festival Award for Best Actress").

first_paragraph: **Tegan Lauren-Hannah Murray** (born 1 July 1989) is an [English](/wiki/English_people "English people") actress. She played [Cassie](/wiki/Cassie_Ainsworth "Cassie Ainsworth") in *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007–2008, 2013) and [Gilly](/wiki/Gilly_(A_Song_of_Ice_and_Fire) "Gilly (A Song of Ice and Fire)") in the [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") fantasy series *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones "Game of Thrones")* (2012–2019), for which she has been nominated along with her castmates for three [Screen Actors Guild Awards](/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Award_for_Outstanding_Performance_by_an_Ensemble_in_a_Drama_Series "Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by an Ensemble in a Drama Series"). Her film roles include the 2014 musical romance film [Stuart Murdoch](/wiki/Stuart_Murdoch_(musician) "Stuart Murdoch (musician)")'s *[God Help The Girl](/wiki/God_Help_the_Girl_(film) "God Help the Girl (film)")* which won her a Special Jury Prize at the [Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival"), and 2015 drama film [Jeppe Rønde](/wiki/Jeppe_R%C3%B8nde "Jeppe Rønde")'s *[Bridgend](/wiki/Bridgend_(film) "Bridgend (film)")* for which she won the [Tribeca Film Festival](/wiki/Tribeca_Film_Festival "Tribeca Film Festival") for [Best Actress Award](/wiki/Tribeca_Film_Festival_Award_for_Best_Actress "Tribeca Film Festival Award for Best Actress").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

English actress

| Hannah Murray | |
| --- | --- |
| Murray at [San Diego Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con") to promote *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones_(TV_series) "Game of Thrones (TV series)")* in 2015. | |
| Born | Tegan Lauren-Hannah Murray[[1]](#cite_note-1)   (1989-07-01) 1 July 1989 (age 36)  [Bristol](/wiki/Bristol "Bristol"), England |
| Alma mater | [Queens' College, Cambridge](/wiki/Queens%27_College,_Cambridge "Queens' College, Cambridge") |
| Occupation | Actress |
| Years active | 2006–present |

**Tegan Lauren-Hannah Murray** (born 1 July 1989) is an [English](/wiki/English_people "English people") actress. She played [Cassie](/wiki/Cassie_Ainsworth "Cassie Ainsworth") in *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")* (2007–2008, 2013) and [Gilly](/wiki/Gilly_(A_Song_of_Ice_and_Fire) "Gilly (A Song of Ice and Fire)") in the [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") fantasy series *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones "Game of Thrones")* (2012–2019), for which she has been nominated along with her castmates for three [Screen Actors Guild Awards](/wiki/Screen_Actors_Guild_Award_for_Outstanding_Performance_by_an_Ensemble_in_a_Drama_Series "Screen Actors Guild Award for Outstanding Performance by an Ensemble in a Drama Series"). Her film roles include the 2014 musical romance film [Stuart Murdoch](/wiki/Stuart_Murdoch_(musician) "Stuart Murdoch (musician)")'s *[God Help The Girl](/wiki/God_Help_the_Girl_(film) "God Help the Girl (film)")* which won her a Special Jury Prize at the [Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival"), and 2015 drama film [Jeppe Rønde](/wiki/Jeppe_R%C3%B8nde "Jeppe Rønde")'s *[Bridgend](/wiki/Bridgend_(film) "Bridgend (film)")* for which she won the [Tribeca Film Festival](/wiki/Tribeca_Film_Festival "Tribeca Film Festival") for [Best Actress Award](/wiki/Tribeca_Film_Festival_Award_for_Best_Actress "Tribeca Film Festival Award for Best Actress").

Between her screen roles she appeared on stage in [Polly Stenham](/wiki/Polly_Stenham "Polly Stenham")'s play *[That Face](/wiki/That_Face "That Face")* in [West End](/wiki/West_End_theatre "West End theatre") (2008) and in the [off West End](/wiki/Off_West_End "Off West End") play *[Martine](/wiki/Jean-Jacques_Bernard "Jean-Jacques Bernard")* (2014).

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hannah_Murray&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Murray was born on 1 July 1989 in [Bristol](/wiki/Bristol "Bristol"). Her parents work at the [University of Bristol](/wiki/University_of_Bristol "University of Bristol"), her father as a professor and her mother as a research technician.[[2]](#cite_note-2) She earned an English degree at [Queens' College, Cambridge](/wiki/Queens%27_College,_Cambridge "Queens' College, Cambridge").[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) She attended North Bristol Post 16 Centre,[[5]](#cite_note-5) and was a member of the Bristol Old Vic Young Company.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hannah_Murray&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

At the age of 16, Murray heard about an audition for young actors in Bristol, and decided to audition for the experience. The auditions were for the [E4](/wiki/E4_(TV_channel) "E4 (TV channel)") teen drama series *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(British_TV_series) "Skins (British TV series)")*. She impressed the producers of the series and was cast as [Cassie Ainsworth](/wiki/Cassie_Ainsworth "Cassie Ainsworth"), a gentle and creative but self-destructive teenager with an [eating disorder](/wiki/Eating_disorder "Eating disorder"). Murray and [April Pearson](/wiki/April_Pearson "April Pearson") were the first two to be cast on the show.[[7]](#cite_note-nationalstudent-7) Murray went on to appear in the first two series, from 2007 to 2008. She left at the end of the show's second series to make way for a new generation of characters. On the decision to replace the cast, Murray has said that "it would be really silly to be in a teenage drama if you're no longer a teenager".[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Following *Skins* in May 2008, Murray made her stage debut as Mia in the critically acclaimed *[That Face](/wiki/That_Face "That Face")*, a [West End](/wiki/London_West_End "London West End") production at the [Duke of York's Theatre](/wiki/Duke_of_York%27s_Theatre "Duke of York's Theatre"). She was highly praised for her acting in the play, and it was considered a milestone in her career.[[9]](#cite_note-9) That same year, she had a small role in the black comedy *[In Bruges](/wiki/In_Bruges "In Bruges")*, but her scene was cut from the film.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f4/SDCC_2015_-_John_Bradley_%26_Hannah_Murray_%2819051560104%29.jpg/250px-SDCC_2015_-_John_Bradley_%26_Hannah_Murray_%2819051560104%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:SDCC_2015_-_John_Bradley_%26_Hannah_Murray_(19051560104).jpg)

Murray (right) and [John Bradley](/wiki/John_Bradley-West "John Bradley-West") (left) at [San Diego Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con") to promote *Game of Thrones*

In 2009, Murray appeared in the [ITV](/wiki/ITV_(TV_network) "ITV (TV network)") adaptation of [Agatha Christie](/wiki/Agatha_Christie "Agatha Christie")'s novel *[Why Didn't They Ask Evans?](/wiki/Why_Didn%27t_They_Ask_Evans%3F "Why Didn't They Ask Evans?")*, playing Dorothy Savage. She also appeared in the thriller film *[Womb](/wiki/Womb_(2010_film) "Womb (2010 film)")* (2010). Later that year, Murray starred in an adaptation of [Enda Walsh](/wiki/Enda_Walsh "Enda Walsh")'s *[Chatroom](/wiki/Chatroom_(film) "Chatroom (film)")*. The film premiered at the 2010 Cannes Film Festival. In early January, she appeared in the British television thriller *Above Suspicion: the Red Dahlia*,[[11]](#cite_note-11) an adaptation of Linda La Plante's novel, in a small role.

On 8 August 2011, [HBO](/wiki/HBO "HBO") confirmed that Murray would portray [Gilly](/wiki/Gilly_(A_Song_of_Ice_and_Fire) "Gilly (A Song of Ice and Fire)") in the second and third seasons of *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones "Game of Thrones")*. She was upgraded to a series regular for the fourth season. Her character is a young woman who has a baby by her own father, and becomes protected by the character [Samwell Tarly](/wiki/Samwell_Tarly "Samwell Tarly").[[12]](#cite_note-12) In 2012, she appeared in the action thriller film *[The Numbers Station](/wiki/The_Numbers_Station "The Numbers Station")*.

In 2013, Murray appeared in a two-episode feature, in the seventh and final series of *Skins*, where she reprised her role as a more serious, solemn, and independent adult Cassie Ainsworth.[[13]](#cite_note-13) She also starred in the music video for "Your Cover's Blown" by Belle & Sebastian.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

In 2014, Murray starred in [*God Help the Girl*](/wiki/God_Help_the_Girl_(film) "God Help the Girl (film)"), about three musicians in [Glasgow](/wiki/Glasgow "Glasgow"). The film premiered at the [Sundance Film Festival](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival") in January 2014, and Murray shared the [World Cinema Dramatic Special Jury Award for the Delightful Ensemble Performance](/wiki/Sundance_Film_Festival "Sundance Film Festival"). Later that year she continued her role as Gilly in *[Game of Thrones](/wiki/Game_of_Thrones "Game of Thrones")* season 4. She also starred in the acclaimed revival of [Jean-Jacques Bernard](/wiki/Jean-Jacques_Bernard "Jean-Jacques Bernard")’s play *[Martine](/wiki/Martine "Martine")*, playing the title role. Her performance was considered one of the most beautiful and heartbreaking of the year by the public and critics, and she was nominated for Best Female Performance at the [Off West End Awards](/wiki/Off_West_End "Off West End").[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Murray starred in *[Lily & Kat](/wiki/Lily_%26_Kat "Lily & Kat")* (2015), an independent American film and the first feature directed by Micael Preysler, about inseparable best friends who struggle to make the best of their last few days together, savouring the city nightlife with an enigmatic artist one of them takes a liking to.

In 2015 she played Sara in the Danish film *Bridgend*, based on the [Bridgend suicides of South Wales](/wiki/Bridgend_suicide_incidents "Bridgend suicide incidents"). The film premiered at the [Rotterdam Film Festival](/wiki/International_Film_Festival_Rotterdam "International Film Festival Rotterdam"), and received positive reviews. The film then had its North American premiere at the [Tribeca Film Festival](/wiki/Tribeca_Film_Festival "Tribeca Film Festival") where it won 3 awards, including Murray for Best Actress.

In 2016 Murray played [Sylvia Ageloff](/wiki/Sylvia_Ageloff "Sylvia Ageloff"), a young [Jewish American](/wiki/Jewish_American "Jewish American") intellectual from [Brooklyn](/wiki/Brooklyn "Brooklyn") and a confidante of Trotsky, in the film *[The Chosen](/wiki/The_Chosen_(2016_film) "The Chosen (2016 film)")*.

In 2017, Murray starred in [Kathryn Bigelow](/wiki/Kathryn_Bigelow "Kathryn Bigelow")'s drama *[Detroit](/wiki/Detroit_(film) "Detroit (film)")*, based on the [Algiers Motel incident](/wiki/Algiers_Motel_incident "Algiers Motel incident") during Detroit's 1967 [12th Street Riot](/wiki/1967_Detroit_riot "1967 Detroit riot"). The film was critically acclaimed.[[16]](#cite_note-DFP-16)

In 2018, she played the lead role of [Leslie "Lulu" Van Houten](/wiki/Leslie_Van_Houten "Leslie Van Houten"), the American convicted [murderer](/wiki/Murder "Murder") and former member of the [Manson Family](/wiki/Manson_Family "Manson Family"), in the film *[Charlie Says](/wiki/Charlie_Says_(2018_film) "Charlie Says (2018 film)")* by [Mary Harron](/wiki/Mary_Harron "Mary Harron"). The movie premiered at the [75th Venice International Film Festival](/wiki/75th_Venice_International_Film_Festival "75th Venice International Film Festival") and was based on the books *The Family*, by [Ed Sanders](/wiki/Ed_Sanders "Ed Sanders"), and *The Long Prison Journey of Leslie Van Houten,* by [Karlene Faith](/wiki/Karlene_Faith "Karlene Faith").[[17]](#cite_note-17)

In January 2026 it was announced that Murray's first book *The Make-Believe* is set for release June 9 2026[[18]](#cite_note-18). The book is an autobiography sharing details of her mental health, involvement with a "wellness organization" and infatuation with its leader. Talking about these events Murray said that "The events of *The Make-Believe* were intensely challenging to live through, but the journey of writing about them has been the most powerfully rewarding thing I've ever known."[[19]](#cite_note-19)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hannah_Murray&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 2024, it was reported as part of the advance publicity for Murray's autobiography that she had been [sectioned](/wiki/Involuntary_commitment "Involuntary commitment") in 2017 following her involvement in a "wellness cult".[[20]](#cite_note-20)[[21]](#cite_note-21)
